words.meaning: happening or existing before now or before another thing
words.example: The previous lesson was easier than this one.
They wore traditional clothes for the festival.
The mountain is visible from our classroom.

words.meaning: very clear, bright, or easy to imagine
words.japaneseMeaning: 鮮やかな、生き生きとした
words.example: The writer gave a vivid description of the market.

words.meaning: being used or busy with an activity
words.example: All meeting rooms were occupied after lunch.

words.japaneseMeaning: 注意深い、気配りのある
words.example: The guide was attentive to every visitor's question.

words.meaning: making someone feel more confident or hopeful
words.japaneseMeaning: 励みになる、勇気づける
words.example: The teacher gave encouraging comments on my essay.
The experience had a profound effect on her.

words.meaning: difficult in a way that tests your ability
words.japaneseMeaning: やりがいのある、難しい
words.example: The course is challenging, but I am learning a lot.

words.meaning: extremely good or clearly better than others
words.japaneseMeaning: 傑出した、非常に優れた
words.example: She gave an outstanding presentation at the contest.
